title: Self-Evolving Codebases
concept: Agent loops that autonomously refactor, test, and optimize their own source code based on continuous performance logs.

exercises.prompt: What do future AI agents rely on to communicate with each other directly?
exercises.explain: Future AI agents use Model-to-Model Protocols to communicate with each other directly.

cards.title: What are Self-Evolving Codebases?
cards.body: Self-evolving codebases are systems where AI agents can improve their own programming automatically, making them smarter over time.

cards.title: Definition of Self-Evolving Codebases
cards.body: Self-evolving codebases use agent loops to refactor, test, and optimize their own source code based on performance data.

cards.title: Analogy: A Recipe that Improves
cards.body: Imagine a chef who edits a recipe each time they cook. They add better ingredients or adjust cooking times based on feedback from diners.

cards.title: Example of Self-Evolving Codebase
cards.body: An AI program that monitors its execution speed may rewrite its own code to run faster based on performance logs.

cards.title: Myth: AI Will Always Need Human Help to Improve
cards.body: While humans can train AI, self-evolving codebases can independently enhance their performance without direct human intervention.

cards.title: Visualizing Self-Evolution
cards.body: Self-evolving codebases constantly analyze data to improve. On the left, a static program; on the right, a self-evolving program that grows smarter.

exercises.prompt: What is a key feature of self-evolving codebases?

options.text: They can optimize their own code based on performance.
They cannot learn from previous actions.

exercises.explain: Self-evolving codebases have the ability to optimize their own code based on performance data, allowing them to enhance functionality.

exercises.prompt: Self-evolving codebases do not utilize performance logs to make improvements.
exercises.explain: This statement is false because self-evolving codebases actively use performance logs to identify areas for improvement.

exercises.prompt: Self-evolving codebases can _______ their own source code based on performance logs.
exercises.sentence: Self-evolving codebases can optimize their own source code based on performance logs.
exercises.explain: The correct term here is 'optimize', as it describes how the codebase enhances itself.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about self-evolving codebases is true?

options.text: They depend entirely on human input for updates.
They automatically refactor and improve their code.

exercises.explain: Self-evolving codebases can automatically refactor and improve their own code without needing constant human input.

exercises.prompt: What does a self-evolving codebase do to improve its performance?

options.text: It rewrites itself based on user commands.
It relies on a fixed set of rules without change.
It uses performance logs to autonomously refactor and optimize its code.
It waits for human developers to make any changes.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that a self-evolving codebase uses performance logs to autonomously refactor and optimize its code. This allows the system to improve continuously without human intervention.
